memoryArtifacts.headline: This building is all that remains of the old Cistercian abbey of Ayes, founded around 1140 by the Dauphine Marguerite of Burgundy.

popularStory.headline: Béatrice de Vienne (1160-1230)
popularStory.description: Béatrice de Vienne ou de Viennois, parfois également nommée de Mâcon, née en 1160 et décédée en 1230, est une noble de la maison de Mâcon, qui régnait également sur Vienne depuis l'an 1100 environ. Pa …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Béatrice Ire de Bourgogne於1143年5月出生在法國布格薩o-吉洛易姆a孔時，她的父親Renaud III comte de Bourgogne50歲，母親Agathe de Lorraine24歲。她於1156年6月17日與Friedrich I. 'Barbarossa' römisch-deutscher Kaiser在德國貝也爾茲包格結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1184年11月15日在法國布格-福朗-可特艾尤拉喬享年41歲，埋葬在德國海藍-普法爾茲斯培也斯培也。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Béatrice Ire de Bourgogne于1143年5月出生在法国布格薩o-吉洛易姆a孔时，她的父亲Renaud III comte de Bourgogne50岁，母亲Agathe de Lorraine24岁。她于1156年6月17日与Friedrich I. 'Barbarossa' römisch-deutscher Kaiser在德国貝也爾茲包格结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1184年11月15日在法国布格-福朗-可特艾尤拉喬享年41岁，埋葬在德国海藍-普法爾茲斯培也斯培也。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bourgogne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Bourgoin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alphee, Alyre, Andre, Armand, Aurore, Celina, Emile, Gilberte, Jacques, Laurent, Lucienne, Olivier.

variant of Bourgouin (and, in North America, an altered form of this), a habitational name for someone from Burgundy (Old French Bourgogne), a region of eastern France having Dijon as its center. The area was invaded by the Burgundii, an ancient Germanic tribe from whom it takes its name, in about AD 480. The duchy of Burgundy, created in 877 by Charles II, King of the Western Franks, was extremely powerful in the later Middle Ages, especially under Philip the Bold (1342–1404; duke from 1363). Compare Bourgoyne and Bourguignon .

nameMeanings.name: Béatrice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: and French form of   Beatrix  , which was quite popular in England during the Middle Ages, and strongly revived in the 19th century. It is most famous as the name of Dante's beloved, and is borne by the elder (  b. 1988 ) of the Duke of York's daughters.

nameMeanings.name: Ire
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: pet form of   Isaac .  It was made famous in the 20th century as the nickname of the American general and president  Dwight D. Eisenhower  (  1890–1969 ), although in this case, of course, it was based on his surname.

variants.name: Isaac
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the son of Abraham, who was nearly sacrificed by his father according to a command of God which was changed at the last moment. A ram, caught in a nearby thicket, was sacrificed instead (Genesis 22:1–13). Isaac lived on to marry Rebecca and become the father of Esau and Jacob. The derivation of the name is not certain; it has traditionally been connected with the Hebrew verb meaning ‘to laugh’. It was borne by both Christians and Jews in the Middle Ages and was taken up by the Puritans in the 16th century.

en.lifeSummary: When Béatrice Ire de Bourgogne was born in May 1143, in Mâcon, Saône-et-Loire, Burgundy, France, her father, Renaud III comte de Bourgogne, was 50 and her mother, Agathe de Lorraine, was 24. She married Friedrich I. 'Barbarossa' römisch-deutscher Kaiser on 17 June 1156, in Würzburg, Bavaria, Germany. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 4 daughters. She died on 15 November 1184, in Jouhe, Jura, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, France, at the age of 41, and was buried in Speyer, Speyer, Rhineland-Palatinate, Germany.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Béatrice Ire de Bourgogne est née en mai 1143, à Mâcon, Saône-et-Loire, Bourgogne, France, son père, Renaud III comte de Bourgogne, avait 50 ans et sa mère, Agathe de Lorraine, avait 24 ans. Elle a épousé Friedrich I. 'Barbarossa' römisch-deutscher Kaiser le 17 juin 1156, à Würzburg, Bayern, Allemagne. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée le 15 novembre 1184, à Jouhe, Jura, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, France, à l’âge de 41 ans, et a été enterrée à Speyer, Speyer, Rheinland-Pfalz, Allemagne.

de.lifeSummary: Als Béatrice Ire de Bourgogne im Mai 1143 in Mâcon, Saône-et-Loire, Burgund, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Renaud III comte de Bourgogne 50 und ihre Mutter Agathe de Lorraine 24. Sie hat am 17. Juni 1156 in Würzburg, Bayern, Deutschland Friedrich I. 'Barbarossa' römisch-deutscher Kaiser geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb am 15. November 1184 im Alter von 41 Jahren in Jouhe, Jura, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, Frankreich und wurde in Speyer, Speyer, Rheinland-Pfalz, Deutschland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Béatrice Ire de Bourgogne è nata il nel maggio 1143 nel Mâcon, Saône-et-Loire, Bourgogne, Francia, sua padre, Renaud III comte de Bourgogne, aveva 50 anni e sua madre, Agathe de Lorraine, aveva 24 anni. Lei sposato Friedrich I. 'Barbarossa' römisch-deutscher Kaiser il 17 giugno 1156, nel Würzburg, Bayern, Germania. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 15 novembre 1184, nel Jouhe, Jura, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, Francia, all'età di 41 anni, e fu sepolta a Speyer, Speyer, Rheinland-Pfalz, Germania.

ja.lifeSummary: Béatrice Ire de Bourgogneが1143年5月にフランスボウルゴグンサoネ-エト-ロイアームaコンで生まれた時，父Renaud III comte de Bourgogneは50歳，母Agathe de Lorraineは24歳であった。1156年6月17日にドイツバイエルンウuルズブルグでFriedrich I. 'Barbarossa' römisch-deutscher Kaiserと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1184年11月15日にフランスボウルゴグネ-フランチェ-コムトeジュラジョウヘで41歳で死去し，ドイツルヘインランド-プファルズスペヤースペヤーにて埋葬された。

ko.Schwaben, Chemnitzer Land, Sachsen, Germ: 독일 작센 크레이스 체니츠-랜드 드u렌울즈도르프 슈와벤
ko.lifeSummary: Béatrice Ire de Bourgogne(이)가 1143년 5월 프랑스 부르고그니 사o니-트-로이르 a콘에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Renaud III comte de Bourgogne(은)는 50세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Agathe de Lorraine(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 1156년 6월 17일 독일 베이른 wu르즈버그에서 Friedrich I. 'Barbarossa' römisch-deutscher Kaiser(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1184년 11월 15일 프랑스 부르고그니-프랭체-코트e 라 조우헤에서 41세의 나이로 사망했으며 독일 리인랜드-p팔즈 스피어 스피어에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Béatrice Ire de Bourgogne nasceu em maio de 1143, em Mâcon, Saône-et-Loire, Bourgogne, França, seu pai, Renaud III comte de Bourgogne, tinha 50 anos e sua mãe, Agathe de Lorraine, tinha 24 anos. Ela casou-se com Friedrich I. 'Barbarossa' römisch-deutscher Kaiser em 17 de junho de 1156, em Würzburg, Bayern, Alemanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu em 15 de novembro de 1184, em Jouhe, Jura, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, França, com 41 anos, e foi sepultada em Speyer, Speyer, Rheinland-Pfalz, Alemanha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Béatrice Ire de Bourgogne родилась май 1143 г. в местности мaкон, саoне-ет-лоир, бургогн, Франция, её отцу по имени Renaud III comte de Bourgogne было 50 лет, а матери, которую звали Agathe de Lorraine, 24. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Friedrich I. 'Barbarossa' römisch-deutscher Kaiser 17 июня 1156 г. в местности вuрзбург, беиирн, Германия. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 4 дочери. Она умерла 15 ноября 1184 г. в местности джоух, джура, бургогне-фрэнче-комтe, Франция в возрасте 41 год и была похоронена в местности спиир, спиир, раинланд-пфалз, Германия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Béatrice Ire de Bourgogne nació en mayo de 1143, en Mâcon, Saône-et-Loire, Bourgogne, Francia, su papá, Renaud III comte de Bourgogne, tenía 50 años y su mamá, Agathe de Lorraine, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Friedrich I. 'Barbarossa' römisch-deutscher Kaiser el 17 de junio de 1156, en Würzburg, Bayern, Alemania. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 15 de noviembre de 1184, en Jouhe, Jura, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, Francia, a la edad de 41 años, y fue sepultada en Speyer, Speyer, Rheinland-Pfalz, Alemania.
